question.text: A trader buys a Nifty put option with strike Rs. 17,500 at a premium of Rs. 177.60. At expiry, Nifty closes at Rs. 17,200. What is the profit per unit?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Put Profit = max(X - S, 0) - Premium
Step-by-step: 1. X = 17,500, S = 17,200, P = 177.60 2. Payoff = max(17,500 - 17,200, 0) = Rs. 300 3. Profit = 300 - 177.60 = Rs. 122.40
**The Trap:** For puts, payoff = X - S (strike minus spot), not S - X.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Put buyer profits when spot FALLS below BEP (X - P = 17,322.40).
**Key Takeaway:** Put Profit = max(X - S, 0) - Premium = 300 - 177.60 = Rs. 122.40.
